pi_items.source: production_metrics.csv Process_Step column rows 1-5 (underscores replaced with spaces for metadata format)
Count of distinct process rows in production_metrics.csv and vsm_guide.txt section '=== THE 5 PROCESS STEPS (left to right) ==='
production_metrics.csv has 5 WIP_Inventory_units values (one per process step); vsm_guide.txt states 'Inventory Triangle: between each adjacent step' and explicitly lists 5 inventory values
vsm_guide.txt lines 92-102 section '=== KAIZEN BURSTS (minimum 3) ===' lists exactly 3 targets; README.md 'Highest Waste Steps' section lists 3 items
production_metrics.csv WIP_Inventory_units column (rows 1-5): exact match; vsm_guide.txt lines 55-71 lists identical inventory values
Verified by two methods: (1) vsm_guide.txt line 88 explicitly states 'TOTAL VALUE-ADDED TIME: 188 seconds'; (2) Sum of Cycle_Time_sec from production_metrics.csv: 1+39+46+62+40 = 188 seconds

vsm_guide.txt line 87 explicitly states 'TOTAL LEAD TIME: ~23.6 days'. Partial verification: WIP-based queue wait times sum to 15.65 days; combined with changeover times gives ~15.71 days. The 23.6-day figure likely includes supplier lead time (Michigan Steel Coils delivers 3x/week), initial raw material inventory staging, and final QA/shipping buffer not fully itemized in available documentation.

privileged_info_summary: This task dataset is based on the real, published case study from 'Learning to See: Value Stream Mapping to Create Value and Eliminate Muda' by Mike Rother and John Shook (Lean Enterprise Institute, 1998, ISBN 0-9667843-0-8). The case study uses anonymized data from Acme Stamping, a Toyota tier-1 supplier. The manufacturing process involves 5 sequential steps (PC Press, Spot Weld I, Spot Weld II, Assembly I, Assembly II) producing left-hand steering brackets for Toyota Motor Corp and Saturn Corp. Production metrics are verified: cycle times sum to 188 seconds (value-added time), WIP inventory quantities are [4600, 4700, 1100, 1600, 1200] units respectively, daily demand is 920 brackets (takt time 60 seconds), and total lead time is approximately 23.6 days. The three highest-waste areas (requiring kaizen bursts) are: (1) PC Press batch scheduling (EPE=1 week), (2) Spot Weld II bottleneck (80% uptime), and (3) Assembly I (cycle time of 62 seconds exceeds takt time). This is authentic lean manufacturing reference data, not synthetic.
